title: Lagoseris marschalliana
taxonomy: Lagoseris marschalliana is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. This plant is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. Within the Asteraceae family, Lagoseris marschalliana is placed in the subfamily Asteroideae, and it belongs to the tribe Senecioneae. The exact classification of Lagoseris marschalliana may be subject to revision as new research emerges. Further study is needed to confirm the current classification of this plant.

intro: Lagoseris marschalliana, also known as Marsh's daisy or Marsh's cotton rose, is a species of flowering plant in the family Compositae. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial effects, making it useful in treating various health issues such as wounds, skin irritations, and infections. The bioactive constituents of Lagoseris marschalliana include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is commonly used to prepare teas, infusions, and topical creams, which are used to treat skin conditions, promote wound healing, and reduce inflammation.
